rulings.Case Title: JERRY B. EPSTEIN VS. ARNOLD SCHWARZENEGGER, GOVERNOR OF THE STATE OF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Leave To Amend Answer To California First Lp'S Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, October 8, 2014, Line 17, DEFENDANTS CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF GENERAL SERVICES and FRED KLASS' Motion For Leave To Amend Answer To California First Lp'S Second Amended Complaint Denied. "[C]ourts are much more critical of proposed amendments to answers when offered¿on the eve of trial." (Hulsey v. Koehler (1990) 218 Cal.App.3d 1150, 1159.) "Where inexcusable delay and probable prejudice to the opposing party is shown," denial of leave to amend is appropriate. (Magpali v. Farmers Group, Inc. (1996) 48 Cal.App.4th 471, 487.) Even if it is assumed that Defendant did not discover the email until April 2014, the evidence establishes that Defendant has inexcusably delayed in bringing this motion. Defendant's counsel admits that he became aware of the emails containing purported misrepresentations in April 2014. (Declaration of Landon D. Bailey, pars. 3-5.) "In order to test the veracity of these written representations" Defendant noticed the depositions of Persons Most Knowledgeable at the investment firms. (Id., pars. 5-8.) These depositions concluded in mid-August. (Id., pars. 6-8.) Defendant offers no explanation of why it waited until September 30, 2014, over a month after the depositions concluded and just two weeks before trial, to seek leave to amend its answer. Further, Plaintiff has shown that it will be unduly prejudiced if leave to amend is granted. (See Declaration of Edward Klein, pars. 13-14.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: MARK EMMETT WILSON VS. MIGUEL ALEJANDRO RESCALVO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Deem Admitted Request For Admission, Set One And Req For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 7, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANT DET NORSKE VERITAS (U.S.A.) INC. Motion To Deem Admitted Request For Admission, Set One And Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalonotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is DENIED. The request for sanctions is denied. Plaintiff provided a declaration stating that he signed the responses on July 28, 2014 and that, through inadvertence, an unsigned version of the responses was served. The Court did not consider the arguments regarding the sufficiency of the responses because the Motion is one to deem admitted requests for admissions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcc@manningllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JASON BISHOP VS. CONSUMERTRACK INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery And Production Of Documents, And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 7, 2014, Line 4, PLAINTIFF JASON BISHOP Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery And Production Of Documents, And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalonotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is GRANTED, as follows: Bishop SI 13-14: All objections are overruled. Defendant to provide code-complaint responses. Bishop RFP 9: All objections are overruled. Defendant to provide code-complaint responses and produce responsive documents. Carper SI 1-2. All objections are overruled. Defendant to provide code-complaint responses. Myers: Defendant to provide amended responses to FI 15.1(c) for each affirmative defense. Myers SI: 1, 2, 8, 9, 11, 18-21, 23-26 and RFP: 4, 5, 6, 8, 14, 15, 17 the objection that these interrogatories are identical to previously served interrogatories is sustained. See Professional Career Colleges, Magna Institute, Inc. v. Sup.Ct. (Stewart) (1989) 207 Cal. App. 3d 490, 494. Myers RFP 1, all objections are overruled. Defendant to provide code-complaint responses and produce responsive documents. The Court rejects defendant's argument that the Motion is untimely based upon (1) the email dated July 23, 2014 offering to toll the motion to compel deadline; and (2) the July 31, 2014 email from Neta agreeing to provide substantive responses. See Neta Decl., Ex. L. Verified amended responses and responsive documents shall be served within two weeks of notice of entry of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcc@manningllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES D SUMNER et al VS. ARA AROUSTAMIAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Change Venue To Los Angeles Superior Court; Request For Plaintiff'S Payment Of Defendant'S Attorney'S Fees Of $1,560.00 And The Costs Of The Transfer
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, October 8, 2014, Line 14, DEFENDANT ARA AROUSTAMIAN's Motion To Change Venue To Los Angeles Superior Court; Request For Plaintiff'S Payment Of Defendant'S Attorney'S Fees Of $1,560.00 And The Costs Of The Transfer Defendant Aroustamian's Motion to Change Venue to Los Angeles Superior Court is denied pursuant to C.C.P. § 395(a). The Complaint adequately pleads a breach of contract cause of action as to Defendant Aroustamian. The San Francisco Superior Court is the proper venue for this action because the parties agreed to adhere to the Confidentiality Agreement in San Francisco County and "the county where the obligation is incurred is the county where it is to be performed." C.C.P. § 395(a). Defendant Aroustamian's request for attorney's fees and expenses is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
